ten4.misc.strict_input.desc: Indicates whether to accept only recipe-related items.
ten4.misc.comparator_mode.output_items.d: Based on how full the output item slots are.
ten4.misc.comparator_mode.output_fluid.d: Based on how full the output fluid tanks are.
ten4.misc.request_interval.desc: The tick interval between each two push and pull.
ten4.misc.facemode.passive_bipass.desc: Passively reacts to input and output requests.
ten4.misc.facemode.passive_input.desc: Passively reacts to input requests. Output is banned.
ten4.misc.facemode.passive_output.desc: Passively reacts to output requests. Input is banned.
ten4.misc.facemode.active_input.desc: Actively pulls and reacts to input requests. Output is banned.
ten4.misc.facemode.active_output.desc: Actively pushes and reacts to output requests. Input is banned.
ten4.pulverizer.desc: Pulverizes ores and metals into dusts, ices into snows, cobbles stones, etc.
ten4.smelter.desc: Uses energy to smelt items. Functionally equivalent to vanilla furnace.
ten4.water_pump.desc: Pumps liquid from the world to its tanks.
item.ten4.energy_coil_augment.desc:
Energy Throughput: x2
Energy Capacity: x2
ten4.guide_landing: Welcome to Technical Engineering 4! This guide will walk you through the basics of energy, items, and fluid logistics, as well as the devices that make it all work.
ten4.category.items.desc: Tools, components, augments, and crafting materials.
ten4.category.devices.desc: Machines for processing, generating, and interacting with the world.
ten4.category.transmission.desc: Ducts for transporting energy, items, and fluids.
ten4.page.wrench0: The $(item)Wrench$(0) is your primary configuration tool.$(br)$(li)Right-click on a duct to open its connection configuration.$(br)$(li)Shift-right-click to instantly remove a duct.$(br)$(li)Right-click on a device face to configure its I/O mode.
ten4.page.circuit0: The $(item)Circuit$(0) is a core crafting component used in all devices. You'll need plenty of these to craft pulverizers, smelters, water pumps, heat generators, and more.
ten4.page.augments0: $(thing)Augments$(0) are upgrade items that can be installed into devices to enhance their performance. Each device has a limited number of augment slots.
ten4.page.augments1: The $(item)Overclocking Augment$(0) increases device power by 25%. Faster processing at the cost of higher energy consumption.
ten4.page.augments2: The $(item)Energy Coil Augment$(0) doubles the energy throughput and energy storage capacity of a device.
ten4.page.augments3: The $(item)Piston Augment$(0) doubles the item throughput, allowing devices to push and pull items twice as fast.
ten4.page.augments4: The $(item)Vacuum Chamber Augment$(0) doubles the fluid throughput, allowing devices to push and pull fluids twice as fast.
ten4.page.materials0: $(thing)Dusts$(0) are produced by pulverizing ores or ingots in a $(l:devices/pulverizer)Pulverizer$(0).$(br)$(thing)Plates$(0) are crafted from ingots using a compressor-like process.$(br)Both dusts and plates are used as ingredients in various crafting recipes.
ten4.page.device_casing0: The $(item)Device Casing$(0) is the foundational block for all TEN4 machines. Craft one to get started, then use it as a base ingredient for pulverizers, smelters, water pumps, and heat generators.
ten4.page.pulverizer0: The $(item)Pulverizer$(0) crushes ores and materials into dusts. It's your primary ore-doubling machine.$(br)$(li)Pulverizes ores into 2 dusts$(br)$(li)Pulverizes ingots into 1 dust$(br)$(li)Crush cobblestone into gravel, gravel into sand$(br)$(li)Crush wool into string, bones into bone meal, and more!$(br)$(br)Requires energy to operate.
ten4.page.pulverizer1: The Pulverizer accepts the following augments:$(br)$(li)$(item)Overclocking Augment$(0) — faster processing$(br)$(li)$(item)Energy Coil Augment$(0) — more energy capacity$(br)$(li)$(item)Piston Augment$(0) — faster item transfer$(br)$(li)$(item)Vacuum Chamber Augment$(0) — faster fluid transfer
ten4.page.smelter0: The $(item)Smelter$(0) uses energy to smelt items, functioning exactly like a vanilla furnace but powered by electricity rather than fuel.$(br)$(br)It accepts the same augments as the Pulverizer for speed and efficiency upgrades.
ten4.page.water_pump0: The $(item)Water Pump$(0) extracts liquid from the world around it and stores it in its internal tanks.$(br)$(br)Place it adjacent to a water source and provide energy to start pumping. It can also extract other fluids from the world if placed correctly.
ten4.page.heat_generator0: The $(item)Heat Generator$(0) produces energy by burning combustible fuels. It's the most basic energy generation device in TEN4.$(br)$(br)Any item that works as furnace fuel can be used here. The generator will automatically output energy to adjacent ducts or devices.
ten4.page.duct_basics0: $(thing)Ducts$(0) are the backbone of your logistics network. They transport energy, items, and fluids between devices.$(br)$(br)There are three variants of each duct type:$(br)$(li)$(item)Normal$(0) — standard duct$(br)$(li)$(item)Opaque$(0) — solid appearance, no internal rendering (better FPS)$(br)$(li)$(item)Controller$(0) — can be configured with a wrench to push/pull
ten4.page.duct_basics1: Use a $(item)Wrench$(0) to configure duct connections. Right-click a duct to open its configuration GUI, where you can set each face to Push, Pull, or Normal mode.$(br)$(br)Ducts automatically connect to adjacent ducts of the same type and to compatible device faces.
ten4.page.energy_duct0: $(thing)Copper Energy Ducts$(0) transfer energy (FE/RF) between devices and energy storage.$(br)$(br)$(li)$(item)Copper Energy Duct$(0) — standard energy transfer$(br)$(li)$(item)Opaque Copper Energy Duct$(0) — solid casing, no internal rendering$(br)$(li)$(item)Copper Controller Energy Duct$(0) — configurable push/pull directions
ten4.page.energy_duct1: Controller ducts can be set to actively push or pull energy using a $(item)Wrench$(0). This allows you to create directed energy flow in your network without needing a separate device to handle routing.
ten4.page.item_duct0: $(thing)Copper Item Ducts$(0) transport items between inventories.$(br)$(br)$(li)$(item)Copper Item Duct$(0) — standard item transport$(br)$(li)$(item)Opaque Copper Item Duct$(0) — solid casing, hidden items$(br)$(li)$(item)Copper Controller Item Duct$(0) — configurable push/pull with filtering
ten4.page.item_duct1: Controller ducts can actively pull items from an inventory or push items into one. Use a $(item)Wrench$(0) to configure the direction and filters.
ten4.page.fluid_duct0: $(thing)Copper Fluid Ducts$(0) transport fluids between tanks and devices.$(br)$(br)$(li)$(item)Copper Fluid Duct$(0) — standard fluid transport$(br)$(li)$(item)Opaque Copper Fluid Duct$(0) — solid casing, hidden fluid$(br)$(li)$(item)Copper Controller Fluid Duct$(0) — configurable push/pull directions
ten4.page.fluid_duct1: Controller ducts can actively push or pull fluids using a $(item)Wrench$(0). This is essential for setting up automated fluid routing between machines.
